title: Dev Plans Page + Stories Skill PLA Workflow
scope: Add a Plans tab under /dev/ that stores plan documents created by the <stories> skill, extend the skill so every invocation drafts a plan, scans existing plans for overlap, and labels every kanban card with its PLA-NNNN identifier. Live state (started/last-updated/finished, acceptance ticks) is reconciled from kanban card transitions.
value: Plans are currently scattered across dev/planning/*.md markdown files with no link to the kanban board cards they spawn. Centralising them in a structured, queryable page (a) gives a single source of truth for what we are building and why, (b) lets the agent confirm whether new work overlaps an existing plan before issuing fresh story IDs, and (c) makes acceptance progress visible without leaving the dev page.
implementation_plan: Pass 1: Extract a shared DevAccordion component, define the PLA-NNNN JSON shape, scaffold the /api/dev/plans GET route, build the Plans tab panel rendering an accordion of plan cards with a sectioned body.

Pass 2: Add docs/c_plan_index.md (PLA counter + registry), extend the stories skill so every run drafts a plan, reaches 95% confidence (asks for web access if needed), searches research papers, scans existing PLA titles + scope for overlap, and adds a PLA-NNNN label to every kanban card.
Pass 3: When a plan accordion opens, fetch the current state of its tagged kanban board cards and recompute date_started / date_last_updated / date_finished plus acceptance criteria ticks on the fly.
feature_list: Plans tab as the first tab under Dev Setup, listing every PLA-NNNN as an accordion row
Plan body rendered with sections 1.0.1 Scope through 10.0.1 References
PLA-NNNN label automatically attached to every story card the stories skill creates
Plan-overlap detection: stories skill scans existing plan titles + scope, asks before merging into an existing plan, otherwise allocates a fresh PLA-NNNN
features_extended: <stories> skill — adds Step -1 (plan draft + 95% confidence loop), Step 0.5 (plan-overlap scan), Step 6.5 (write plan JSON), and a new PLA-NNNN mandatory label on every card
Shared DevAccordion replaces the inline accordion in DevReportsPanel and DevResearchPanel — visual parity preserved

work_item_backlog.title: Plans tab + DevAccordion extraction
work_item_backlog.notes: Pass 1 of this plan; lives in this same conversation.

work_item_backlog.title: Pull-on-load kanban board sync for plan progress

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Opening the Plans tab lists every PLA-NNNN file in dev/plans/ as an accordion row
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: DevPlansPanel renders an accordion populated from /api/dev/plans GET

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Expanding a plan row shows sections 1.0.1 through 10.0.1 with the JSON content rendered
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Plan body component renders scope, value, implementation_plan, areas_impacted, feature_list, work_item_backlog, acceptance_criteria, risks, references

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Every card created by <stories> carries a PLA-NNNN label matching the plan it belongs to
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Stories skill Step 5c verification list includes the PLA label; rerun fails if missing

acceptance_criteria.criterion: When the user invokes <stories> for work that touches an existing plan, the skill confirms the match before proceeding
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Skill output names the matched PLA-NNNN, summarises overlap, and asks the user to approve merging into the existing plan vs creating a new one

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Plan dates and acceptance ticks reflect the current kanban card states
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Pull-on-load: opening a plan row triggers a fetch of its tagged kanban board cards; date_started / date_finished / acceptance done flags recompute from card list states

risks.risk: Plan-overlap scan could produce false positives, blocking new work behind a phantom existing plan
risks.mitigation: Skill always presents the matched plan to the user for confirmation before merging; user can reject and allocate a fresh PLA-NNNN.

risks.risk: PLA-NNNN counter drift if multiple agents create plans in parallel
risks.mitigation: Same approach as story IDs — read docs/c_plan_index.md AND scan dev/plans/ for the highest existing PLA-NNNN; use max+1.
